description_long: This object consists of two parallel rows of hexagonal dumbbells, each row containing five dumbbells of increasing size. The left row features a rusty, brownish surface, while the right row is a more uniform gray, suggesting different material finishes or aging. Each dumbbell has a straight, cylindrical handle connecting two large hexagonal end caps, which provide stability and prevent rolling. The surfaces of the dumbbells show visible signs of wear, with the left row exhibiting rust and discoloration, while the right row appears more matte and less corroded. The hexagonal end caps are flat-faced, and some faint markings are visible on the ends, though they are not clearly legible. The overall arrangement suggests these dumbbells are intended for strength training and could be found in urban gyms, garages, or outdoor fitness areas.
description: A set of two rows of hexagonal dumbbells, one rusty brown and one gray, each with cylindrical handles and flat hexagonal end caps, showing signs of wear and use.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the hexagonal shapes of the dumbbell end caps and the size progression in each row.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the cylindrical handles and the parallel arrangement of the two rows, with clear color and surface differences.
description_view_2: Reverse top-down view emphasizing the flat faces of the end caps and the alignment of the dumbbells in each row.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the handles and the contrasting surface conditions between the two rows.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical handle, likely round in cross-section, appears smooth with no visible knurling or ergonomic features.
attribute_end_cap_design: Hexagonal, flat-faced end caps made of metal, with visible wear, rust, and some faint markings; no decorative patterns.
attribute_condition: Worn and aged, with visible rust, discoloration, and surface scratches, especially on the left row; right row appears less corroded but still used.
